feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Process Integration
feed.subtitle: SAP Process Integration Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: SFTP Error: promptPassword(String message) is not implemented yet!" after SP28 installed
entry.content:
Hi,

We had recently updgraded SAP PO system to SP28. For one of the SFTP receiver channel, I am receving the following error:

"Message could not be forwarded to the JCA adapter. Reason: java.lang.UnsupportedOperationException: promptPassword(String message) is not implemented yet!" 

I had already verified the SAP Note: 3406752 and applied the following Additional parameter in the SFTP Receiver Channel -> Advanced mode

customPreferredAuthentication  = "password"

But still the error remains same.

Any help would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you,

Vijay

entry.title: SAP PO - Rest Sender with oAuth2
entry.content:
Hi

I can setup rest Sender in SAP PO with the SAP PO server itself as a Authorization server at the URL - <pohost>/RESTAdapter/OAuthServer/

But is it possible to use an external Authorization server? Means login.microsoftonline.com.... As the token validator?

entry.title: Extra line added to XI output after file is sent from SAP via proxy
entry.content:
We are sending a XML file from SAP to XI via proxy. When the file is opened at XI end they are getting an additional empty line or LF(Line feed) after each item. This file when sent further is not processed due to this empty line.

On checking the payload in SXMB_MONI for the interface there is not empty line item added while sending.

How can we identify why this empty line is getting added for every item.

File opened at XI side: Line 3 and 5 is empty with LF and continues for all the items.

entry.title: SAP PI/PO SFTP to SFTP pass trough scenario UNIX to WINDOWS (LF to CRLF)
entry.content:
Hello Community.

We have a pass trough scenario (there is no conversion) from SFTP UNIX to SFTP WINDOWS.
The expected line sapartor in the target file is CRLF:

 

In File adapter there is a parameter (File Type) where we can set the 'Text' value and it's automatically handled by the adapter to use CRLF.
In SFTP adpter there is no this parameter available.

We have being trying to use different EJB modules (SAP XI Sample/ConvertCRLFfromToLF, MessageTransformBean and TextCodepageConversionBean) with no sucess.

Could you please provide any experience you had with this situation and how it was solved.

I appreciate any comments/help.

 

Thank you!
Fellipe

entry.title: Azure Databricks Connection using SAP PO
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Does anyone have experience on integrating Azure Databricks with S4 using SAP PO/PI?

We have requirement to pull the delta data from Azure databricks Database to SAP S4 system. We also have SAP PO 7.5 single stack in our landscape for this purpose.. SAP S4 system is expecting the data to be transformed in to an IDOC structure.. So, the question is what would be state of art technology to implement this interface? Should we ask Azure Databricks to execute Query statement to fetch the data from various tables and store it on Azure Databricks temporary storage somewhere and if it is possible to store the data on Azure databricks on some temporary storage? if yes, what should be that temporary storage option available on Azure Databricks side? 

1. SAP PO fetches the data from Azure Databricks and then it transforms the data to IDOC & post it to S4?

(or)

2. SAP PO directly polls the database tables using simple query statement? 

Which approach do you think is the best approach?

Thanks,

Ramu.

entry.title: What is the best structure for organizing integration classes via PI/PO proxy?
entry.content:
I have a task to check the organization of PI classes in an environment.

The classes generated for proxy interfaces are organized as subclasses of the same class. So, when a new interface is created, instead of a new class being created for this interface, the same class destined for that namespace is used, and then only one new method is created, meaning that some objects can be reused.

This is generating several problems in the environment, because when two developers need to work at the same time on two different interfaces, a dependency occurs and one of them cannot proceed.

The choice to do the architecture this way was made a long time ago, and I imagine it will take a lot of work to undo it. However, I would like to know, in your opinion, what is the best way to organize these classes?

I usually make a class for each interface, but I'm open to ideas.

entry.title: Username Change in Incoming IDOC
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts ,

For incoming IDOC from CPI , the username is not  getting changed from custom user name  (R_xxxx) to WF-BATCH at status 62 (IDOC passed to application) with the processing program SAPLBD20 . Due to that  custom user ID ( System User type ),  Authorization is failing to create the document . This is happening all of sudden.

Expectation is that it should have WF-BATCH as user .

I have checked all the possibilities but couldn't find what could be the cause of this issue . Please let me know if this can be  due to any enhancement or IDOC configuration for that Partner profile/ message type.

entry.title: Fetch data from multiple tables, then store it and compare it and send IDOCs to S4
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Is it possible to fetch the data from multiple tables of database? and then store that data temporarily somewhere and then send only delta load data as Proxy messages to S4? or should we ask the sender database guys to build stored procedure kind of thing on database on their side and then SAP PO JDBC adapter calls that stored procedure to fetch the data from database? or Kindly let me know the best solution to solve this requirement.. thank you!

Below diagram is not perfect but the requirement is fetch data from multiple tables of Database and then pass it to S4?

 

Thanks

Ramu

entry.title: Flat file to hierarchy target mapping
entry.content:
Hi,

I am using PI 7.4 and using graphical mapping.

My source is a flat file which I have map it to a hierarchical output based on line number value.

Basically DocumentLineNumber can be repeated based on LotNumber values.

INPUT

<row>
<DocumentNumber>11</DocumentNumber>
<DocumentLineNumber>10</DocumentLineNumber>
<ItemNumber>MAT-123</ItemNumber>
<LotNumber>lot1</LotNumber>
<TransactionQuantity>1</TransactionQuantity>
</row>
<row>
<DocumentNumber>11</DocumentNumber>
<DocumentLineNumber>10</DocumentLineNumber>
<ItemNumber>MAT-123</ItemNumber>
<LotNumber>lot2</LotNumber>
<TransactionQuantity>2</TransactionQuantity>
</row>
<row>
<DocumentNumber>11</DocumentNumber>
<DocumentLineNumber>20</DocumentLineNumber>
<ItemNumber>MAT-343</ItemNumber>
<LotNumber>lot3</LotNumber>
<TransactionQuantity>5</TransactionQuantity>
</row>
<row>
<DocumentNumber>11</DocumentNumber>
<DocumentLineNumber>30</DocumentLineNumber>
<ItemNumber>MAT-742</ItemNumber>
<LotNumber>lot4</LotNumber>
<TransactionQuantity>4</TransactionQuantity>
</row>

Desired Output

 <Request>
      <DocumentNumber>11</DocumentNumber>
      <lines>        
         <DocumentLineNumber>10</DocumentLineNumber>
         <ItemNumber>MAT-123</ItemNumber>
         <lotItemLots>
            <LotNumber>lot1</LotNumber>
    <TransactionQuantity>1</TransactionQuantity>
         </lotItemLots>
         <lotItemLots>
            <LotNumber>lot2</LotNumber>
    <TransactionQuantity>2</TransactionQuantity>
         </lotItemLots>
       </lines>
 
       <lines>        
         <DocumentLineNumber>20</DocumentLineNumber>
         <ItemNumber>MAT-343</ItemNumber>
         <lotItemLots>
            <LotNumber>lot3</LotNumber>
    <TransactionQuantity>5</TransactionQuantity>
         </lotItemLots>
       </lines>
 
       <lines>        
         <DocumentLineNumber>30</DocumentLineNumber>
         <ItemNumber>MAT-742</ItemNumber>
         <lotItemLots>
            <LotNumber>lot4</LotNumber>
            <TransactionQuantity>4</TransactionQuantity>
         </lotItemLots>
       </lines>
   </Request>
 
How do we convert this in graphical mapping?
Thanks

entry.title: JDBC sender to REST receiver synchronous message
entry.content:
Hi,

I am using PI 7.4

My scenario is JDBC sender ( Stored Procedure is executed in the channel ) to REST receiver. I need to capture the response of REST service and update back a table in JDBC.

I have created mapping and IC with Response message mapping in REST receiver channel.

When running the scenario, REST service is called but the response message is not being captured.

When I am trying to give 'AF_Modules/ResponseOnewayBean' in the sender JDBC, it is giving error 'null object loaded from local variable 'message1'.

Please help on how to configure JDBC to REST synchronous scenario.

Thanks

entry.title: API-To retrieve ICOs from PO system
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I'm looking a way to retrieve all my ICOs from PI/PO system. what are the available ways to achieve it?

Best Regards,
Madhu

entry.title: SHA-512 error in CPI but works in groovyide
entry.content:
Hi All,

The below code works fine in groovy ide and i am trying to convert a string to SHA-512 encoded string.

Error received in SAP CPI is 

No signature of method: java.lang.String.digest() is applicable for argument types: (java.lang.String) values: [SHA-512]
Possible solutions: getAt(java.lang.String), next(), toSet(), size(), size(), toList()

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message; 
import java.util.HashMap; 
import java.security.MessageDigest; 
import java.security.*
import java.text.DateFormat; 
import java.text.SimpleDateFormat; 
import java.util.Date; 
import java.time.*
def Message processData(Message message) {
    def props = message.getProperties();
    def origDate = props.get("date")
    Date oldDate = Date.parse('yyyy-MM-ddHH:mm:ss', origDate);
    long datetimestampUnix = oldDate.getTime();
    datetimestampUnix = datetimestampUnix/1000
 message.setHeader("iat", datetimestampUnix)
 def body = datetimestampUnix+"ClientID"+"Password"+"ClientSecret"+"username"
 message.setHeader("Hash", body)
 body = body.get
      def sha512 = body.digest('SHA-512')
     message.setHeader("SHA-512", sha512)
	return message;
}

entry.title: How to convert the http URL to https secure protocol?
entry.content:
How to convert the http URL to https secure protocol in SAP PI/PO system.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Automation Of IDoc Metadata Deletion / Refresh In NWA
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community!
There is a need in my team to automate the process of IDoc Metadata Deletion / Refresh in SAP PO nwa.
Do you know any way to do so? - Maybe with an Integration scenario / REST adapter / Python script.
Meanwhile we do it manually through - NWA -> SOA -> Monitoring -> IDoc Adapter Monitor -> Metadata Monitor -> and then you choose Delete / Reload to the IDoc Type you want.
We use SAP Netweaver 7.5 and our SAP PO is single java stack.

Thank you in advance!!
We'll be very happy for a solution 🙂

entry.title: Databricks DB issue SAP PO
entry.content:
Hi All,

We are working on Interface to poll the Data from Databricks(Azure) DB and the connection work fine .. SAP PO JDBC sender adapter is able to fetch the data .. We also executed a simple Query SELECT TABLENAME and it also works fine.. But the issue is when executing a big Query statement...

When the Query statement is directly executed on Azure Databricks DB and it works fine..

Please see below screenshot where it shows the results when executed on Databricks DB..

But JDBC sender channel throws error issue when trying to execute a big Query statement.

Please see below screenshot where it shows the results when executed on Databricks DB.

 

Could you please suggest some inputs to resolve this issue?

Thanks

Ramu

entry.title: SAP PI/PO (If Message enter in SAP PI system how message will further Proceed)?
entry.content:
Dear experts,

I have a question. One of my interviewers asked me, "The message has entered in your SAP Pi system. What will you do?" like what will we further process.

I'm new to this field, so I don't understand the question. Could you please help me figure out what response he was expecting from me?

I told him that if the target side wants a message of this type, we will map it accordingly and move forward with the message. Was this the right answer?

Thanks,

Sanju.

entry.title: URL giving empty response from POSTMAN/CPI but working fine with Browser
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

When I am trying to use the URL from browser to get the details, it is working as expected(No Authentication Required). But, when trying to get the details from POSTMAN it is showing an empty response [].

Similar is the case with CPI HTTP adapter as well.

Please suggest, where I am going wrong. I tried other methods apart from GET but nothing working.

Thanks,

Nidhi

entry.title: How to handle x-www-form-urlencoded Content-Type header in SAP PI
entry.content:
Hi experts

I have a proxy to REST integration where I have the following header :

Content-Type: application/x-www-formurlencoded

This Integration is to be used to generate an access toke. The Request Body has the client-id and the client-secret. I am using the following blog for this without the dynamic URL part because I am working with a static URL.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/handling-x-www-form-urlencoded-format-using-rest-adapter/ba-p/13387849 

However I am unable to add the java mapping in the ESR because the jar file attached to the blog is not available anymore. This is also the first time I am working with java mapping and I am honestly quite lost. Most tutorials I have come across seem to be using Netweaver Developer Studio which I am unable to download because I do not have a S-user id. I am also not familiar with importing jar files and most sites I have found do not have anything pointing to those available for SAP PI 7.50 which I am using.

So, I was wondering if it is possible to handle this with a UDF? Also if it is, Do I need to create new data types or message types for this? Also, please guide me on how to write the UDF. I am very new to this so I would really appreciate it if someone could provide me a detailed answer or a link to a blog that provides a detailed answer. I have also gone through this blog without success.

Thank you

entry.title: Custom Error handling in UDF, SAP PI/PO
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

We currently have a integration in place that processes price files from vendors. During this process, we perform a lookup to identify any changes in price between the data in our ERP system and the newly received price files. If no price difference is detected, an exception is currently being thrown.

As expected, this results in a large number of errors in the PI/PO message monitor, especially given the volume of vendors we work with. I'm seeking a way to modify this functionality so that instead of throwing an error, we can simply log a message such as 'No price changes detected' without registering it as an error in the message monitor. Is this even possible?

if (recs == 0) {
throw new RuntimeException("EXCEPTION ERROR IN PRICE CONDITION FILTER, ZERO RESULT RECORDS ! ");
}

 Any ideas or possible solutions would be greatly appreciated!

Thanks.

entry.title: SAP PO FILE adapter additional file not keeping original file type in MAIL adapter as attachment
entry.content:
I have FILE to MAIL scenario. File sender is processing two file FileToMail.mail and FileToMail.csv. The first one serves as mail body and second one as attachment processed by additional files option in file adapter.

The issue is that FileToMail.csv is attaching as FileToMail.xml and i dont know why. I cant find the reason why is it changing its extension from .csv to .xml and tried many things to fix it without success.

Here is my file sender:

and here is my mail receiver:

I upload these two files into ftp

and result is always this. Mail body correctly processed but attachment with wrong extension:
